entry.title: Atlas M0
entry.description: Atlas M0 is MongoDB's free cloud database tier for small-scale development and prototyping.

freeTierDetails.summary: Free, always-on MongoDB cluster with limited resources for development and learning.

quickstartSteps: Sign up for a MongoDB Atlas account.
Create a new project and select the M0 free tier cluster.
Choose your preferred cloud provider and region (from available free options).
Configure cluster settings and deploy.
Add database users and whitelist your IP address.
Connect to your cluster using the provided connection string.
entry.whenToUse: Use Atlas M0 for learning MongoDB, prototyping, or developing low-traffic apps without production requirements.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid Atlas M0 for production workloads, high-traffic apps, or when you need backups, custom networking, or higher performance.
entry.body: Atlas M0 provides a free, managed MongoDB cluster suitable for learning, prototyping, and small-scale development, but is not intended for production use.

entry.title: Atlassian Jira Free
entry.description: Jira Free offers cloud-based project and issue tracking for small teams with essential agile features and integrations.

freeTierDetails.summary: Free for up to 10 users with core Jira Software features and limited storage.
caveats: No audit logs or SLA features

quickstartSteps: Sign up at the Atlassian Jira website.
Create a new Jira Software project.
Invite up to 9 additional team members.
Set up your first board (Kanban or Scrum).
Start creating and assigning issues.
entry.whenToUse: Use Jira Free for small teams needing agile project tracking and collaboration with basic automation and integrations.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you need advanced permissions, more than 10 users, or enterprise-level support and security features.
entry.body: Atlassian Jira Free provides essential project tracking and agile tools for small teams, with a 10-user limit and basic automation features.

entry.title: Azure Cosmos DB
entry.description: Globally distributed, multi-model NoSQL database service with low latency and elastic scalability from Microsoft Azure.

freeTierDetails.summary: Always-free tier with 400 RU/s provisioned throughput and 25 GB storage per month.
limits: One free database account per Azure subscription
caveats: Free tier must be selected at account creation
billingRiskNotes: Usage above free tier limits is billed at standard rates

quickstartSteps: Sign in to the Azure Portal and create a new Cosmos DB account, selecting the free tier option.
Choose your preferred API (Core SQL, MongoDB, Cassandra, Gremlin, or Table).
Configure region(s) for global distribution if needed.
Create a database and container within your account.
Connect to Cosmos DB using the SDK or REST API from your application.
entry.whenToUse: Use when you need a globally distributed, low-latency NoSQL database with flexible data models and automatic scaling.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you require a traditional relational database, or if your workload consistently exceeds the free tier limits.
entry.body: Azure Cosmos DB offers a globally distributed, multi-model NoSQL database with low latency, elastic scalability, and a generous always-free tier for small workloads.

entry.title: Azure PostgreSQL Flexible Server
entry.description: Managed PostgreSQL database service with flexible scaling, high availability, and built-in security on Azure.

freeTierDetails.summary: 750 hours/month of Burstable B1ms instance, 32 GB storage, and 32 GB backup storage.
caveats: Free tier only available in select regions
Only one free server per Azure subscription
billingRiskNotes: Exceeding limits incurs standard pay-as-you-go charges

quickstartSteps: Sign in to the Azure portal.
Navigate to 'Create a resource' and select 'Azure Database for PostgreSQL Flexible Server'.
Choose the 'Burstable B1ms' compute tier and configure storage within free limits.
Select a supported region and complete required settings.
Review and create the server.
Connect to your PostgreSQL server using provided connection details.
entry.whenToUse: Use when you need a managed PostgreSQL database with minimal setup and predictable free-tier limits for development or small workloads.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you require advanced performance, large-scale production, or multi-region deployments beyond free-tier constraints.
entry.body: Azure PostgreSQL Flexible Server offers a managed PostgreSQL experience with a generous free tier, ideal for development, testing, and small production workloads. The always-free tier provides enough resources for most entry-level use cases, but users should be aware of regional availability and the single-server-per-subscription limit. Exceeding the free tier's compute or storage limits will result in charges, so monitor usage closely if running persistent or growing workloads.

entry.title: Azure SQL Database
entry.description: Managed relational database service for scalable, high-availability SQL workloads on Azure.

freeTierDetails.summary: 250 GB S0 database free for 12 months with Azure Free Account.
billingRiskNotes: Service stops if free tier is exceeded during trial period

quickstartSteps: Sign up for an Azure Free Account.
Navigate to Azure Portal and select 'Create a resource'.
Choose 'SQL Database' and configure database settings.
Select the S0 pricing tier and review free tier eligibility.
Deploy the database and connect using Azure SQL tools.
entry.whenToUse: Use when you need a managed SQL Server database for development, prototyping, or learning on Azure with minimal setup.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you need always-free production databases, require non-SQL engines, or want to avoid time-limited free tiers.
entry.body: Azure SQL Database offers a managed, scalable SQL Server experience with a 12-month free S0 database for new Azure Free Account users.

entry.title: CockroachDB Cloud
entry.description: CockroachDB Cloud offers a fully managed, distributed SQL database with PostgreSQL compatibility and high resilience.

freeTierDetails.summary: Free tier includes a single cluster with up to 5GB storage and limited compute resources.
caveats: Compute and storage limits are enforced.
Free clusters may be paused after 60 days of inactivity.

quickstartSteps: Sign up for a CockroachDB Cloud account.
Create a new free cluster from the dashboard.
Configure network access and obtain connection string.
Connect using a PostgreSQL-compatible client.
Start creating databases and tables.
entry.whenToUse: Use when you need a resilient, scalable SQL database with minimal operational overhead and PostgreSQL compatibility.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you need advanced PostgreSQL extensions, require on-premise deployment, or need large-scale production clusters without cost.
entry.body: CockroachDB Cloud provides a managed, distributed SQL database service with a generous free tier suitable for learning, prototyping, and small-scale applications.

entry.title: DynamoDB
entry.description: AWS DynamoDB is a fully managed NoSQL database service for fast and flexible performance at scale.

freeTierDetails.summary: Free tier includes 25 GB storage and up to 200 million requests per month.
caveats: Free tier valid for 12 months after AWS account creation
On-demand capacity mode not included in free tier
billingRiskNotes: Overages are billed at standard rates if limits are exceeded

quickstartSteps: Sign up for an AWS account and log in to the AWS Management Console.
Navigate to DynamoDB and click 'Create table'.
Enter a table name and primary key, then choose default settings.
Review and create the table.
Use the AWS SDK or Console to add and query items.
entry.whenToUse: Use DynamoDB for scalable, low-latency NoSQL workloads with unpredictable traffic or when you need serverless database management.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you need complex relational queries, multi-table joins, or strong ACID transactions across tables.
avoidIf: You have strict relational data needs
entry.body: DynamoDB is AWS's fully managed NoSQL database service, offering seamless scalability, high performance, and serverless operation for modern application workloads.

entry.title: EverSQL
entry.description: EverSQL is an online platform for automatic SQL query optimization and indexing recommendations.

freeTierDetails.summary: Free tier allows limited SQL query optimization and index recommendations.
limits: Up to 5 query optimizations per month
caveats: Advanced features require a paid plan
Query length and complexity may be restricted

quickstartSteps: Sign up for a free EverSQL account.
Log in and navigate to the query optimization tool.
Paste your SQL query into the editor.
Click 'Optimize' to receive recommendations.
Review suggested changes and apply them to your database.
entry.whenToUse: Use EverSQL when you need quick, automated SQL query optimization or index suggestions without deep database expertise.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you require unlimited optimizations, advanced tuning, or support for highly complex enterprise workloads.
entry.body: EverSQL provides an easy way to optimize SQL queries and receive index recommendations, making it suitable for developers and teams seeking quick performance improvements without deep database tuning knowledge.

entry.title: Firebase Firestore
entry.description: A scalable, serverless NoSQL document database for building web and mobile apps in real time.

freeTierDetails.summary: Free Spark plan includes limited reads, writes, and storage per month.
caveats: Limits are per project and reset daily.
No guaranteed SLA on Spark plan.

quickstartSteps: Create a Firebase project at the Firebase Console.
Add Firestore to your project and select the Spark (free) plan.
Register your app (web, iOS, or Android) in the Firebase Console.
Install the Firebase SDK in your app.
Initialize Firestore in your code and start reading/writing data.
entry.whenToUse: Use Firestore when you need a serverless, real-time NoSQL database for web or mobile apps with moderate usage.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you require complex queries, strict relational data, or need to exceed free tier limits without incurring costs.
entry.body: Firebase Firestore is a fully managed, serverless NoSQL document database that enables real-time data sync for web and mobile applications. Its generous free tier is ideal for prototyping, student projects, and small-scale production apps, but be mindful of daily operation limits and lack of advanced relational features.

entry.title: Firebase Realtime Database
entry.description: A cloud-hosted NoSQL database for building real-time, collaborative applications with automatic data synchronization.

freeTierDetails.summary: The Spark plan offers a free tier with limited database usage and connections.
caveats: Limited to one project per account
billingRiskNotes: Service stops when free limits are reached

quickstartSteps: Create a Firebase project at the Firebase Console.
Add Firebase Realtime Database to your project.
Configure database rules for your use case.
Integrate the Firebase SDK into your app.
Read and write data using the provided API.
entry.whenToUse: Use when you need real-time data sync for web or mobile apps with minimal backend setup.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you require complex queries, large-scale commercial use, or need SQL-like relational features.
entry.body: Firebase Realtime Database provides a simple, scalable NoSQL backend for apps needing live data updates, ideal for rapid prototyping and learning.

entry.title: MongoDB Atlas
entry.description: MongoDB Atlas is a fully managed cloud database service for deploying, managing, and scaling MongoDB clusters.

freeTierDetails.summary: Free shared cluster with limited resources and features.

quickstartSteps: Sign up for a MongoDB Atlas account.
Create a new free M0 cluster.
Configure cluster region and settings.
Add a database user and set access rules.
Connect to the cluster using your preferred driver.
entry.whenToUse: Use when you need a managed MongoDB database for learning, prototyping, or small-scale applications without infrastructure overhead.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you require high performance, large storage, advanced features, or dedicated resources for production workloads.
entry.body: MongoDB Atlas offers a free shared cluster tier suitable for learning, prototyping, and small projects, with easy setup and no credit card required.

entry.title: Neon
entry.description: Neon is a serverless Postgres platform with autoscaling, branching, and a generous free tier for developers.

freeTierDetails.summary: Free tier includes 10 GB storage, 3 branches, and 500,000 Postgres compute seconds per month.
caveats: No custom VPC or private networking on free tier

quickstartSteps: Sign up at neon.tech using your email or GitHub account.
Create a new project and select your region.
Configure your database and create your first branch.
Copy the connection string and use it in your application.
Monitor usage and manage branches from the Neon dashboard.
entry.whenToUse: Use Neon for rapid development, prototyping, or when you need serverless Postgres with easy branching and no infrastructure management.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid Neon if you require advanced networking, high-throughput workloads, or strict on-premise compliance requirements.
entry.body: Neon provides a modern, serverless Postgres experience with branching and autoscaling, making it ideal for development, testing, and small production workloads.

entry.title: Pinecone
entry.description: Pinecone is a managed vector database for building fast, scalable similarity search and AI applications.

freeTierDetails.summary: Free tier offers a single pod with 1GB storage for development and testing.
caveats: Limited performance and features compared to paid plans

quickstartSteps: Sign up for a Pinecone account.
Create a new project and select the free Starter environment.
Set up your first index with default settings.
Integrate Pinecone SDK in your application.
Insert and query vectors using the API.
entry.whenToUse: Use Pinecone when you need a managed vector database for prototyping or small-scale AI search and similarity applications.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid Pinecone if you need high-throughput, production SLAs, or advanced features not available in the free tier.
entry.body: Pinecone provides a fully managed vector database for fast similarity search, ideal for prototyping AI and semantic search applications with a generous free tier.

entry.title: PlanetScale
entry.description: PlanetScale offers a serverless MySQL-compatible database platform with horizontal scaling and non-blocking schema changes.

freeTierDetails.summary: Free tier includes 1 production database with generous storage and connection limits.

quickstartSteps: Sign up at planetscale.com with your GitHub account.
Create a new database from the dashboard.
Connect using the provided credentials and a MySQL client or ORM.
Deploy schema changes using PlanetScale's branching workflow.
Start building and querying your application.
entry.whenToUse: Use PlanetScale when you need a scalable, managed MySQL database with zero-downtime schema changes and a generous free tier.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you require advanced MySQL features like triggers, or need private networking and backups on the free plan.
entry.body: PlanetScale provides a serverless, MySQL-compatible cloud database with a generous free tier, ideal for prototypes, learning, and small production workloads. The platform supports branching workflows for schema changes, but the free plan lacks backups and private networking. It's well-suited for developers seeking managed SQL without operational overhead.

entry.title: Qdrant
entry.description: Qdrant is a vector database service for storing, searching, and managing embeddings at scale.

freeTierDetails.summary: Free tier includes 1GB storage, 1M operations/month, and 1 project.
caveats: No SLA or support on free tier

quickstartSteps: Sign up for a free Qdrant Cloud account.
Create a new project and collection.
Connect using the Qdrant client or REST API.
Upload your vector embeddings.
Run similarity search queries.
entry.whenToUse: Use Qdrant when you need fast, scalable vector search for AI-powered applications or semantic search features.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid Qdrant if you need traditional relational queries or require on-premise deployment without using their open-source version.
entry.body: Qdrant provides a managed vector database for storing and searching embeddings, suitable for AI-driven search and recommendation use cases.

entry.title: Redis
entry.description: Redis is an in-memory data store used for caching, real-time analytics, and fast key-value operations.

freeTierDetails.summary: Redis Cloud offers a free tier with limited memory and throughput for development and small workloads.

quickstartSteps: Sign up for a Redis Cloud account at redis.com.
Create a new free database instance.
Copy the connection endpoint and credentials.
Install a Redis client library in your application.
Connect to your Redis instance and start storing data.
entry.whenToUse: Use Redis when you need fast, in-memory data storage for caching, session management, or real-time analytics.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid Redis for large, persistent datasets or when you require strong consistency and durability guarantees.
entry.body: Redis provides a fast, in-memory key-value store ideal for caching, session management, and real-time analytics, with a free cloud tier for small workloads.

entry.title: Redis Cloud
entry.description: Fully managed Redis service offering scalable in-memory data storage and caching with a generous always-free tier.

freeTierDetails.summary: Always-free tier with 30 MB storage and basic features.
caveats: Limited to one free database per account

quickstartSteps: Sign up for a Redis Cloud account.
Create a new free database instance.
Select your preferred cloud provider and region.
Copy the database endpoint and credentials.
Connect your application using a Redis client.
entry.whenToUse: Use when you need a managed, low-latency cache or key-value store for small apps, prototypes, or learning purposes.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you require high availability, clustering, or need more than 30 MB storage for production workloads.
entry.body: Redis Cloud provides a fully managed, always-free Redis database ideal for caching, session storage, and rapid prototyping with minimal setup.

entry.title: Sqlable
entry.description: Sqlable offers free, serverless SQL databases with instant setup and a generous always-free tier.

freeTierDetails.summary: Always-free tier with 1 database, 10,000 rows, and 100,000 queries per month.
caveats: No custom domains on free tier
Limited support for advanced SQL features

quickstartSteps: Sign up at sqlable.com with your email.
Create a new database from the dashboard.
Define your tables and schema using the web UI or SQL editor.
Connect your app using the provided API endpoint and credentials.
Start running queries and building your application.
entry.whenToUse: Use Sqlable for quick prototypes, student projects, or small apps needing a free, serverless SQL database with minimal setup.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid Sqlable if you need advanced SQL features, high performance, custom domains, or production-grade SLAs.
entry.body: Sqlable provides a simple, always-free serverless SQL database ideal for prototypes, learning, and small projects, with hard limits and no credit card required.

entry.title: Supabase Database
entry.description: Managed PostgreSQL database with instant REST and realtime APIs, authentication, and storage, designed for modern app development.

freeTierDetails.summary: Free tier includes a managed PostgreSQL database with generous usage limits for small projects.

quickstartSteps: Sign up for a free Supabase account.
Create a new project and choose a database password.
Access the project dashboard and note your API keys.
Use the SQL editor to create tables or import data.
Connect your app using the provided REST or client libraries.
entry.whenToUse: Use when you need a managed PostgreSQL database with instant APIs and authentication for small to medium projects.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you require large-scale production workloads, advanced database features, or custom backup and recovery options.
entry.body: Supabase Database offers a managed PostgreSQL instance with built-in APIs, authentication, and storage, ideal for quickly launching and iterating on modern web and mobile applications.

entry.title: Upstash Redis
entry.description: Serverless Redis database with per-request pricing and a generous free tier for low-latency caching or session storage.

freeTierDetails.summary: Free tier includes 10,000 requests/day, 256 MB storage, and 1 database.
caveats: Hard cap on requests and storage
No VPC peering on free tier

quickstartSteps: Sign up for a free Upstash account.
Create a new Redis database from the dashboard.
Copy the database endpoint and credentials.
Connect using your preferred Redis client or SDK.
Start reading and writing key-value data.
entry.whenToUse: Use when you need a simple, serverless Redis instance for caching, session storage, or lightweight data needs with minimal setup.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you require advanced Redis modules, high-throughput workloads, or private networking features not available in the free tier.
entry.body: Upstash Redis offers a serverless, pay-per-request Redis database with a generous free tier, ideal for lightweight caching, session storage, and prototyping without infrastructure management.
